content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

February 7, 2017

Gary S. Winuk
Kaufman Legal Group
A Professional Corporation
1201 K Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-17-024

Dear Mr. Winuk:

This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of the San Diego Unified Port
District (the "Port") and Port Commissioner Robert Nelson regarding the conflict of interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Please note we are not a finder of fact when
rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes your
facts are accurate and complete.

Please also note that the Commission does not advise with respect to past conduct.
(Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A).) Therefore, nothing in this letter should be construed to evaluate any
conduct that may have already taken place, and any conclusions contained in this letter apply only
to prospective actions.

In correspondence dated January 23, 2017, you confirmed that this request is solely for
advice regarding the Act and not Section 1090. Accordingly, please also note that this advice does
not pertain to any other area of law outside the Act, including Section 1090, the Public Contract
Code, or common law conflicts of interest.

QUESTIONS

1. Is the San Diego County Taxpayers Association ("SDCTA") a financial interest of the
Commissioner under the Act as a result of its receipt of $50,000 for ballot measure campaign
services from SDCTA Political Action Committee ("SDCTA PAC")?

2. Are the Lodging Industry Association PAC ("LIA PAC"), the Infrastructure PAC of the
Associated General Contractors ("Infrastructure PAC"), or Bartell Hotels, financial interests of the
Commissioner?

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File No. A-17-024
Page No. 2

3. Does the Commissioner have a conflict of interest under Section 84308 on account of the campaign contributions to the SDCTA PAC?

4. Is GafCon a financial interest of the Commissioner as a result of Josh Gaffen's service as an uncompensated member of the SDCTA's Board of Directors?

CONCLUSIONS

Based on the facts presented:

1. Yes. The SDCTA and/or SDCTA PAC are financial interests of the Commissioner for 12 months following his final receipt of income from SDCTA and/or SDCTA PAC.

2. No. The LIA PAC, Infrastructure PAC, and Bartell Hotels are not financial interests of the Commissioner at this time by virtue of their contributions to SDCTA PAC.

3. No. Section 84308 does not apply to the Commissioner because there is no indication that the Commissioner "accepted, solicited, or directed a contribution" from any party or participant or an agent of either.

4. No. Neither Mr. Gaffen nor GafCon are financial interests of the Commissioner as a result of Mr. Gaffen's service as an uncompensated member of the SDCTA's Board of Directors.

FACTS

You are the authorized representative of the Port and Port Commissioner Robert Nelson. The Commissioner was appointed to the Port Commission in 2011 and has served in that role continuously since then.

Manalatos Nelson Murphy (hereafter "MNM Public Relations"), a San Diego-based advertising and public relations limited liability company, was established in July 2016. The Commissioner owns a 29 percent stake in MNM Public Relations. MNM Public Relations has numerous clients, including some entities that engage in political activity. In August 2016, the SDCTA, a nonprofit corporation, became a client of MNM Public Relations.

In October 2016, the SDCTA PAC hired MNM Public Relations to provide campaign advertising services related to ballot measures in the City of Chula Vista, City of San Diego, Cuayamaca-Grossmont College District, and Grossmont Unified School District appearing on the November 8, 2016 election ballot. SDCTA PAC is a California campaign committee organized on May 14, 1998 (FPPC ID# 981406) to support and oppose local ballot measures in the San Diego area.

As disclosed on the SDCTA PAC Form 460 campaign disclosure statement filed with the San Diego County Registrar of Voters for the period of July 1 to October 22, 2016, SDCTA PAC received campaign contributions from the following entities in the following amounts:

File No. A-17-024
Page No. 3

NAME OF CONTRIBUTOR                                    AMOUNT

LIA PAC                                                $80,000
Infrastructure PAC                                     $10,000
Bartell Hotels                                         $43,350

In turn, the LIA PAC received the following contributions during the 2015-16 election cycle from contributors who are under the Port's jurisdiction:

NAME OF CONTRIBUTOR                                    AMOUNT

Bartell Hotels                                         $18,350
Hilton Worldwide, Inc.                                 $38,250
Marriott International, Inc.                          $33,900
Sheraton San Diego Hotel & Marina                      $26,325
Manchester Grand Hyatt San Diego                       $40,625

The LIA PAC also received over $100,000 in contributions from contributors who are not subject to the Port's jurisdiction. You state that none of the contributions to SDCTA PAC or LIA PAC were solicited by SDCTA PAC or LIA PAC or earmarked by the contributors to pay for or hire MNM Public Relations in any way.

The services relating to the November 8, 2016 ballot measures that MNM Public Relations provided to SDCTA PAC were limited to advertising and did not involve fundraising for SDCTA PAC or any other entity. You state that: MNM Public Relations received in excess of $50,000 from SDCTA PAC for those services; MNM Public Relations has paid a majority of those funds to sub-vendors; and those payments will be disclosed by SDCTA PAC in due course.

Richard Bartell is the president of Bartell Hotels and one of its owners, and Bartell Hotels contributed to both the SDCTA PAC and the LIA PAC.

Hilton Worldwide, Inc. was a contributor to the LIA PAC but was not a contributor to the SDCTA PAC. One Park Boulevard, LLC (hereafter "OPB"), doing business as Hilton San Diego Bayfront Hotel, holds a long-term lease as a tenant of the Port. OPB is a partnership in which Hilton Worldwide, Inc. owns a twenty-five percent interest. OPB is currently seeking approval from the Port for (1) a change of ownership structure of a subtenant, Sunstone Park Lessee, LLC, and (2) a 13-year agreement between that subtenant and HLT Conrad Domestic LLC, a wholly owned subsidiary of Hilton Worldwide, Inc., extending the agreement through December 31, 2046. These potential lease modifications will require approval by the Port Commission, which will consider the modifications on February 7, 2016.

Josh Gaffen is the Vice President of Client Development for GafCon and serves as an uncompensated member of the SDCTA Board of Directors. The SDCTA Board of Directors is currently comprised of 45 members, including Mr. Gaffen. You state that Mr. Gaffen does not participate in SDCTA PAC decisions, and that GafCon is not a contributor to either the SDCTA PAC or LIA PAC.

File No. A-17-024
Page No. 4

ANALYSIS

Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or using
his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which the official has a financial
interest. A public official has a "financial interest" in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that
the decision will have a material financial effect on one or more of the official's interests set forth
in Section 87103. Section 87103 identifies the following interests:

• Any business entity in which the official has a direct or indirect investment worth $2,000 or
more. (Section 87103(a).)

• Any real property in which the official has a direct or indirect interest worth $2,000 or more.
(Section 87103(b).)

• Any source of income, except gifts or loans by a commercial lending institution made in the
regular course of business on terms available to the public without regard to official status,
aggregating $500 or more in value provided or promised to, received by, the official within
12 months prior to the time when the decision is made. (Section 87103(c).)

• Any business entity in which the official is a director, officer, partner, trustee, employee, or
holds any position of management. (Section 87103(d).)

• Any donor of, or any intermediary or agent for a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating $470 or
more in value provided to, received by, or promised to the official within 12 months prior to
the time when the decision is made. (Section 87103(e).)

• The official's personal finances, including those of the official's immediate family.

SDCTA and/or SDCTA PAC

The Commissioner has financial interests in SDCTA and SDCTA PAC as sources of income
because each of those entities have provided income to MNM Public Relations within the past 12
months. Accordingly, based on the facts presented, we conclude that the SDCTA and/or SDCTA
PAC are financial interests of the Commissioner under the Act for 12 months following his final
receipt of income from SDCTA and/or SDCTA PAC. However, there are no decisions presented
that would have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on SDCTA or SDCTA PAC,
therefore the Commissioner would not have a conflict of interest by virtue of his interest in SDCTA
and/or SDCTA PAC. (See Regulation 18701(b).)

LIA PAC, Infrastructure PAC, and Bartell Hotels

The Act's definition of a "gift" and its definition of "income" expressly exclude reportable
campaign contributions; therefore, even a contribution made directly to an elected official would

File No. A-17-024
Page No. 5

not be a financial interest.² (Sections 82028(b)(4) and 82030(b)(1).) In this case, the contributors in
question made contributions to the SDCTA PAC, and therefore are even further attenuated from the
Commissioner. Accordingly, based on the facts presented, we conclude that contributors to the
SDCTA PAC are not financial interests of the Commissioner under the Act.³

Section 84308

Section 84308 states that "no officer of any agency shall accept, solicit, or direct a
contribution of more than two hundred fifty dollars ($250), from any party, or his or her agent, or
from any participant, or his or her agent, while a proceeding involving a license, permit, or other
entitlement for use is pending before the agency and for three months following the date a final
decision is rendered in the proceeding if the officer knows or has reason to know that the participant
has a financial interest . . ." (Section 84308(f) [discussing prohibitions and
disqualifications]; see Section 84308(d) [describing how similar prohibitions are enforced against
parties, participants, and their agents].)

There is no indication in the facts presented that the Commissioner "accepted, solicited, or
directed a contribution" from any party or participant or an agent of either. Accordingly, Section
84308 is not applicable to the Commissioner.

Mr. Gaffen and/or GafCon

A board member of a nonprofit organization will not be considered a source of income to
the official based solely on his or her organization being a source of income to the official unless:
(1) the member or members at issue are financially affected by the decision at issue, and (2) the
member or members actually control the nonprofit organization's decisions. (Farrell Advice Letter,
No. 1-02-227 citing to Lucas Advice Letter, No. A-99-059.)

Because there is no indication in the facts presented that Mr. Gaffen actually controls the
nonprofit organization SDCTA or the SDCTA PAC's decisions, we do not pierce the SDCTA
Board, and Mr. Gaffen is not a source of income to the Commissioner under the Act. Therefore,
based on the facts presented, we conclude that Mr. Gaffen and GafCon are not financial interests of
the Commissioner under the Act at this time.

² The California Supreme Court has recognized that the Act's definition of "financial interest" expressly
excludes an elected official's receipt of campaign contributions. (Woodland Hills Residents Assn., Inc. v. City Council
(1980) 26 Cal.3d. 938, 945-946; accord All Towing Services LLC v. City of Orange (2013) 220 Cal.App.4th 946, 955.)
As the Supreme Court has explained, the Act "provides for disclosure of campaign contributions by recipients of
contributions rather than disqualification of recipients from acting in matters in which the recipient is interested.
(Woodland Hills Residents Assn., Inc., supra, at p. 945.)

³ You have also asked whether the Act prohibits the Commissioner from taking part in decisions relating to the
potential modification of the long-term lease between One Park Boulevard, LLC and the Port (due to Hilton
Worldwide, Inc. having an ownership interest in One Park Boulevard, LLC), given that Hilton Worldwide, Inc. is a
contributor to the LIA PAC. Because the Commissioner is not financially interested in the contributors to the LIA PAC,
including Hilton Worldwide, Inc., the Act does not prohibit the Commissioner from taking part in those decisions, so
long as the Commissioner is not otherwise financially interested in Hilton Worldwide, Inc.

File No. A-17-024
Page No. 6


If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Hyla P. Wagner
General Counsel

[signature]

By: Matthew F. Christy
Counsel, Legal Division

MFC:jgl

sections.analysis:
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or using 
his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which the official has a financial 
interest. A public official has a "financial interest" in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that 
the decision will have a material financial effect on one or more of the official's interests set forth 
in Section 87103. Section 87103 identifies the following interests: 
• Any business entity in which the official has a direct or indirect investment worth $2,000 or 
more. (Section 87103(a).) 
• Any real property in which the official has a direct or indirect interest worth $2,000 or more. 
(Section 87103(b ). ) 
• Any source of income, except gifts or loans by a commercial lending institution made in the 
regular course of business on tenns available to the public without regard to official status, 
aggregating $500 or more in value provided or promised to, received by, the official within 
12 months prior to the time when the decision is made. (Section 87103(c).) 
• Any business entity in which the official is a director, officer, partner, trustee, employee, or 
holds any position of management. (Section 87103(d).) 
• Any donor of, or any intennediary or agent for a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating $470 or 
more in value provided to, received by, or promised to the official within 12 months prior to 
the time when the decision is made. (Section 87103(e).) 
• The official's personal finances, including those of the official's immediate family. 
SDCTA and/or SDCTA PAC 
The Commissioner has financial interests in SDCT A and SDCT A PAC as sources of income 
because each of those entities have provided income to MNM Public Relations within the past 12 
months. Accordingly, based on the facts presented, we conclude that the SDCT A and/or SDCT A 
PAC are financial interests of the Commissioner under the Act for 12 months following his final 
receipt of income from SDCT A and/or SDCT A PAC. However, there are no decisions presented 
that would have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on SDCT A or SDCT A PAC, 
therefore the Commissioner would not have a conflict of interest by virtue of his interest in SDCT A 
and/or SDCTA PAC. (See Regulation 18701(b).) 
LIA PAC. Infrastructure PAC. and Bartell Hotels 
The Act's definition of a "gift" and its definition of "income" expressly exclude reportable 
campaign contributions; therefore, even a contribution made directly to an elected official would  
not be a financial interest. 2 (Sections 82028(b )( 4) and 82030(b )( l ). ) In this case, the contributors in 
question made contributions to the SDCT A PAC, and therefore are even further attenuated from the 
Commissioner. Accordingly, based on the facts presented, we conclude that contributors to the 
SDCT A PAC are not financial interests of the Commissioner under the Act.3 
Section 84308 
Section 84308 states that "no officer of any agency shall accept, solicit, or direct a 
contribution of more than two hundred fifty dollars ($250), from any party, or his or her agent, or 
from any participant, or his or her agent, while a proceeding involving a license, permit, or other 
entitlement for use is pending before the agency and for three months following the date a final 
decision is rendered in the proceeding if the officer knows or has reason to know that the participant 
has a financial interest . . .. " (Section 84308(b); Regulation 18438.7 [discussing prohibitions and 
disqualifications]; see Section 84308(d) [describing how similar prohibitions are enforced against 
parties, participants, and their agents].) 
There is no indication in the facts presented that the Commissioner "accepted, solicited, or 
directed a contribution" from any party or participant or an agent of either. Accordingly, Section 
84308 is not applicable to the 9ommissioner. 
Mr. Gaffen and/or GafCon 
A board member of a nonprofit organization will not be considered a source of income to 
the official based solely on his or her organization being a source of income to the official unless: 
(I) the member or members at issue are financially affected by the decision at issue, and (2) the 
member or members actually control the nonprofit organization's decisions. (Farrell Advice Letter, 
No. l-02-227 citing to Lucas Advice Letter, No. A-99-059. ) 
Because there is no indication in the facts presented that Mr. Gaffen actually controls the 
nonprofit organization SDCTA or the SDCT A PAC's decisions, we do not pierce the SDCT A 
Board, and Mr. Gaff en is not a source of income to the Commissioner under the Act. Therefore, 
based on the facts presented, we conclude that Mr. Gaffen and GafCon are not financial interests of 
the Commissioner under the Act at this time. 
2 The California Supreme Court has recognized that the Act's definition of"financial interest" expressly 
excludes an elected official's receipt of campaign contributions. ( Woodland Hills Residents Assn., Inc. 1•. City Council 
(1980) 26 Cal.3d. 938, 945-946; accord All Towing Sen•ices LLC v. City of Orange (2013) 220 Cal.App.4th 946, 955.) 
As the Supreme Court has explained, the Act "provides for disclosure of campaign contributions by recipients of 
contributions rather than disqualification of recipients from acting in matters in which the recipient is interested. 
(Woodland Hills Residents Assn., Inc., supra, at p. 945.) 
l You have also asked whether the Act prohibits the Commissioner from taking part in decisions relating to the 
potential modification of the long-term lease between One Park Boulevard, LLC and the Port (due to Hilton 
Worldwide, Inc. having an ownership interest in One Park Boulevard, LLC), given that Hilton Worldwide, Inc. is a
